skill.name: tone-adjuster
skill.description: Converts medical text between academic and patient-friendly tones while preserving clinical accuracy. Translates medical jargon for patients, adapts research papers for public audiences, and rewrites clinical notes for patient handouts.
skill.summary: Bidirectional medical text tone converter that preserves clinical accuracy while adjusting readability level.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 43 static findings from the automated scanner were evaluated as false positives. The 33 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' alerts at SKILL.md lines 22-252 are markdown code fence backticks (```), not Ruby backtick shell operators. The 2 'hardcoded URL' findings at SKILL.md:7 refer to a legitimate GitHub source attribution link. The 3 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings and 4 'system reconnaissance' findings are scanner hallucination on non-cryptographic, non-reconnaissance content. The skill is a legitimate medical text converter with no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown code fences misidentified as shell backtick execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 33 locations of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. All flagged lines are markdown code fence backticks (```) used for code block formatting in Python, bash, JSON, and text code blocks. These are not Ruby backtick shell execution operators. Example: lines 33-37 show a bash code block with 'cd' and 'python' commands inside markdown fences, not shell backtick execution. No actual command injection or shell execution risk exists.

low_findings.title: Source repository URL misidentified as hardcoded URL risk
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:7 for 'hardcoded URL'. This is a GitHub repository source attribution link: https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills. It is a standard source citation, not an exfiltration endpoint or malicious network call.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:24, and audit JSON:62,77. These lines contain YAML frontmatter description text, documentation text about consistent output, and JSON audit commentary about agent usability and maintainability. No cryptographic operations exist in any skill file.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'system reconnaissance' at references/guidelines.md:9, SKILL.md:54, SKILL.md:66, and audit JSON:23. These reference Flesch-Kincaid readability tests (a standard readability formula), documentation about output discipline, and audit commentary about scientific integrity. No system reconnaissance intent or capability exists.

content.user_title: Adjust Medical Tone Between Academic and Patient-Friendly Language
content.value_statement: Medical professionals often struggle to communicate complex clinical information to patients in an understandable way. This skill converts medical text between academic rigor and patient-friendly language while preserving clinical accuracy.
actual_capabilities: Converts academic medical text to patient-friendly language at specified reading levels
Translates complex medical jargon into plain language equivalents
Assesses reading level and jargon density of medical content
Converts patient descriptions into precise clinical terminology
Provides structured output with assumptions, risks, and limitations clearly stated
limitations: Cannot provide medical advice or interpret clinical data
Reading level assessment uses standard formulas, not clinical validation
May not preserve all nuances of highly specialized medical subfields
Does not support languages other than English for medical conversion

use_cases.title: Research Publication Translation
use_cases.description: Researcher converts a published clinical study into a patient-friendly summary for a hospital newsletter.

use_cases.title: Clinical Note Simplification
use_cases.description: Physician rewrites discharge notes with complex terminology into a clear patient handout.

use_cases.title: Medical Education Content
use_cases.description: Medical educator creates accessible learning materials from advanced textbook content.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Patient-Friendly Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this medical text to patient-friendly language: [insert medical text]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick translation of a short medical phrase or sentence for immediate patient communication

prompt_templates.title: Reading-Level Targeted Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the following clinical note to a patient handout at 8th-grade reading level. Replace all medical jargon with plain language. Keep the key medical facts intact: [insert clinical note]
prompt_templates.scenario: Healthcare provider creates patient-facing materials at a specific literacy level

prompt_templates.title: Bidirectional Medical Translation with Quality Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this patient description and convert it to clinical terminology suitable for a medical chart note. Then provide the reading level assessment of both the original and converted text: [insert patient description]
prompt_templates.scenario: Medical scribe converts patient-reported symptoms into clinical documentation

prompt_templates.title: Full Tone-Adjustment Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the tone-adjuster skill, complete a full workflow: 1) Assess the reading level of the input text, 2) Convert it to the specified target tone, 3) Provide a jargon translation table, 4) List any medical accuracy risks in the conversion. Input: [insert text]. Target tone: [patient-friendly or academic].
prompt_templates.scenario: Medical communication specialist needs comprehensive tone analysis and conversion

output_examples.input: The patient presents with acute myocardial infarction and requires immediate percutaneous coronary intervention.
output_examples.output: The patient is having a heart attack and needs emergency treatment to open the blocked blood vessel.

output_examples.input: My chest has been hurting for three days and I feel dizzy when I stand up.
output_examples.output: The patient reports three days of chest pain and orthostatic dizziness, which may indicate reduced cardiac output.

best_practices: Always specify the target reading level for consistent output: 5th-6th grade, 8th grade, 12th grade, or college.
Verify critical medical facts are preserved after conversion by comparing key terms between the original and translated versions.
Use academic-to-patient direction for clinical communication and patient-friendly-to-academic for clinical documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to interpret medical test results or provide diagnostic information.
Do not omit caveats or risk disclaimers when simplifying complex medical information for patients.
Do not convert text without first confirming the intended audience and their preferred language and literacy context.

faq.question: What reading levels does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports 5th-6th grade for young patients, 8th grade for general adult audiences, 12th grade for educated lay audiences, and college level for healthcare professionals.

faq.question: Can this skill translate medical text to other languages?
faq.answer: No. This skill only converts between academic and patient-friendly English. It does not translate between different languages.

faq.question: Is the converted output medically accurate?
faq.answer: The skill preserves the original medical facts during conversion. However, you should verify critical terms and dosages before sharing with patients.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for clinical decision-making?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for communication and documentation purposes only. It does not provide clinical decision support or diagnostic capabilities.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access?
faq.answer: No. The conversion is performed by the AI based on its training. No external API calls or network access is needed.

faq.question: Can this skill process large medical documents?
faq.answer: It works best with individual paragraphs or sections. Very large documents may need to be processed in smaller segments for best results.
